title: Senator Gallivan Calls for Delay in Implementation of Advanced Clean Truck Regulations in New York
content:
Senator Patrick M. Gallivan, (R-C, Elma) is urging Governor Kathy Hochul to delay adoption and impending implementation of the Advanced Clean Truck (ACT) regulations by the Department of Environmental Conservation (DEC).
First adopted in 2021 as part of the stateâ€™s Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act (CLCPA), the ACT rule will require manufacturers to produce and sell an increasing percentage of zero-emission trucks and buses starting in 2025. The regulation would require that medium and heavy-duty vehicles, including municipal plows, be manufactured as zero emission, with the goal of 100% of trucks over 8,500 pounds to be zero emission by 2045 release.
author: Analysts Account
